secondary_keyword: how much does a puppy cost in Singapore

dual_intent_strategy.primary_intent: commercial — buyer comparing puppy prices across Singapore shops, deciding where to buy
dual_intent_strategy.secondary_intent: informational — searcher researching what a puppy costs in Singapore before committing
dual_intent_strategy.resolution: Lead with Curious Tails' transparent $2,888–$4,488 all-inclusive range and value bundle (transactional), then give the honest market-wide cost picture and first-year budget education (informational). The page answers 'how much?' for the whole market, then shows why the all-in price here is the safer comparison basis.

meta_title: Puppy Pricing Singapore: $2,888–$4,488 All-Inclusive | Curious Tails
meta_description: How much does a puppy cost in Singapore? Transparent pricing from $2,888–$4,488 — every puppy includes a 30+ item starter kit ($500+ value), free home delivery & setup, and a free AVS-certified training lesson. WhatsApp us for current availability.
h1: Transparent Puppy Pricing Singapore — Everything Included

entity_definitions.definition: Curious Tails is a licensed pet shop in Balestier, Singapore (AVS licence AS24J00046) selling puppies at a transparent $2,888–$4,488 — every price includes a 30+ item starter kit, free home delivery and setup, and a free AVS-certified training lesson.

Puppy pricing in Singapore ranges from about $800 to over $13,900 depending on breed, size, coat and where you buy. At AVS-licensed shops, the price should also reflect what is included — health records, licensing paperwork, and after-sale support — not just the puppy itself.

serp_analysis.dominant_content_format: cost-of-ownership blog guides + pet shop listing pages
serp_analysis.estimated_average_word_count: 1,200–2,000 for cost guides; 400–800 for listing pages
serp_analysis.search_intent_confirmation: Mixed commercial-informational: buyers want a concrete number for their budget AND a trustworthy place to buy; DataForSEO confirms 'puppy price singapore' (170/mo) is commercial with informational foreign intent.

featured_snippet.puppy pricing Singapore: none — AI Overview occupies the slot; target a 40–55 word paragraph answer capsule
featured_snippet.how much does a puppy cost in Singapore: paragraph — target paragraph format in a DefBox within first 30% of page

serp_analysis.knowledge_panel: LocalBusiness schema with priceRange $2,888–$4,488 and sameAs to AVS public registry reinforces entity
top_competitor_weaknesses: Insurers' cost guides never say what a licensed shop price includes
Shop listing pages show numbers with zero inclusions breakdown
No competitor explains WHY prices vary $800–$13,900
Nobody covers deposit, boarding, and delivery logistics costs
No first-year budget bridged to an actual purchase decision
consensus_topics_low_gain: annual food, vet, grooming cost estimates
information_gaps_none_cover: line-item breakdown of what an all-inclusive puppy price contains (kit, delivery, setup, training)
free boarding after deposit until the family is ready
licensing paperwork completed at point of purchase by the shop
what happens if the puppy falls sick in the first 5 days
why licensed-shop prices differ from Carousell/Telegram 'bargains' in real risk dollars
vagueness_gaps: "Prices vary by breed" → no explanation of size, coat, age, demand and licensing-cost drivers
"Corgis cost S$7,880–8,500" → no note that Corgis are not HDB-approved, which changes the buying decision
"Budget S$800–9,000 to buy" → no guidance on what SHOULD be included at each price level
unanswered_followup_questions: What do I actually get for the price at a licensed shop?
Do I pay extra for delivery, licence paperwork, or first vaccinations?
How much do I need in month one AFTER buying the puppy?
Can the shop hold the puppy if my flat isn't ready yet?
sourcing_transparency_gaps: Listing pages never explain where puppies come from or why that affects price
No competitor publishes a price range commitment; most say 'WhatsApp for price'
regulatory_compliance_gaps: AVS dog licence requirement and who handles it at purchase is absent from all cost guides
Pet Ownership Course requirement for first-time owners never mentioned alongside costs
cro_conversion_gaps: Cost guides have zero conversion path (insurer blogs)
Shop pages use 'call for price' — friction for price-researching buyers
No trust signals (licence number, reviews) near price figures anywhere
No mid-page CTA between education sections
decision_stage_gaps: No 'is this price fair?' checklist for a buyer holding a quote
No reassurance about what happens after payment (boarding, delivery timing, sick-puppy support)
factual_conflicts: "Cavapoo $4,300–$6,500 (Stage-1 market data)" vs "OnlyPaws Maltipoo from $1,999 (SERP listing)" → resolution: market spread is real; position $2,888–$4,488 as transparent mid-range with $500+ of inclusions, cheaper than main competitors (Wag a Tail SGD 3,500–7,800 live SERP evidence).

faq.question: How much does a puppy cost at Curious Tails?
faq.answer_direction: Every puppy is between $2,888 and $4,488, and the price always includes the 30+ item starter kit ($500+ value), free home delivery with playpen setup, a free AVS-certified training lesson, and licensing paperwork completed at purchase. Exact price depends on the individual puppy — WhatsApp 82206480 for current litters.

faq.question: Why don't you list an exact price for each puppy?
faq.answer_direction: Individual pricing varies with breed, coat and age, and availability changes weekly. Publishing the committed $2,888–$4,488 range keeps us honest while WhatsApp gives you the live number for the specific puppy you're asking about.

faq.question: Do you charge extra for delivery, setup or the training lesson?
faq.answer_direction: No — home delivery, playpen setup at your home, and the first AVS-certified training lesson are all included in the listed price. There are no add-on fees at collection.

faq.question: How much should I budget for the first year beyond the purchase?
faq.answer_direction: Roughly $1,200–$3,500: AVS licence from $15/year, food $340–$2,430/year depending on size, vet care $300–$500/year, grooming around $274/year. The starter kit removes the ~$500 of day-one equipment from that list.

faq.question: Why are your puppies $500–$2,000 cheaper than the big-name shops?
faq.answer_direction: We spend almost nothing on marketing and pass that on. Every licensed shop meets the same AVS standards — we compete on what's included and the aftercare, not ad budgets.

faq.question: What happens if my puppy falls sick soon after coming home?
faq.answer_direction: If your puppy falls sick within the first 5 days of going home, bring it back to the shop and we'll nurse it. After that, we remain a WhatsApp away for any care question — support doesn't end at the sale.

faq.question: Can you hold my puppy if my home isn't ready yet?
faq.answer_direction: Yes — after your deposit, we board your puppy free (lodging and food covered) until you're ready, then schedule delivery around your availability, usually mornings or evenings.

faq.question: Is adopting cheaper than buying?
faq.answer_direction: Yes — adoption packages in Singapore run about $70–$400 and are the honest answer for tight budgets. If you want a specific small breed with structured support, a licensed shop is the alternative; ours is $2,888–$4,488 all-in.
